message:
fix(btree): correct index leaf page handling during B-tree rebalancing

The balance_nonroot algorithm was incorrectly treating index leaf pages
the same as table leaf pages when gathering and distributing cells
during a page split or merge. This caused index entries to be silently
dropped because divider cells (which contain unique index keys) were
not being gathered into the cell array for index leaves -- only for
interior pages.

Changes to balance.rs:
- When gathering cells from sibling pages, index leaf (LeafIndex) pages
  now have their parent divider cells collected. The 4-byte left-child
  pointer is stripped from the InteriorIndex divider to convert it into
  a proper LeafIndex cell before inclusion in the gathered cell array.
- compute_distribution() now receives the full BtreePageType instead of
  a boolean is_leaf flag. This allows it to distinguish LeafTable from
  LeafIndex: only LeafTable uses the leaf distribution algorithm, while
  LeafIndex uses the interior distribution (which accounts for divider
  cell consumption between pages).
- When emitting divider cells back to the parent after redistribution,
  the code now correctly prefixes the consumed cell with a 4-byte child
  pointer for index leaves, matching the InteriorIndex cell format.
- The cell_cursor skip logic after distributing cells to a page now
  advances past the consumed divider for both interior pages AND index
  leaves (previously only interior pages).
- Comments updated throughout to clarify the distinction between
  LeafTable (dividers extracted from rowids) and LeafIndex (dividers
  are promoted key cells that must be preserved).

Changes to cursor.rs:
- binary_search_index_interior() no longer returns Found -- the
  comparison is changed from a three-way Ordering match to a two-way
  less-than check, so equal keys go right (lo = mid + 1). This is
  correct for SQLite's index B-tree semantics where interior cells are
  boundary markers, not exact-match targets; the actual leaf cell must
  be found by descending to the appropriate child.
- seek_index_key() removes the Found branch from the interior-page
  search result match, replacing it with an unreachable!() assertion
  since the binary search can no longer return Found.

These changes fix corruption that could occur when an index B-tree
underwent a page split: missing divider cells meant the index would
lose entries, producing incorrect query results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
